codeReview.summary: The preset run command is fully implemented with parameter interpolation, connection resolution, and all required flags. The code follows project conventions for output format, error handling, and Go style. All 13 expected behaviors from the feature description are correctly implemented. The tests cover the main scenarios including SQL injection detection, type validation, and connection resolution.

issues.description: No test specifically exercises the --output-file flag functionality. While the executePresetRun helper supports outputFile parameter, no test case passes a non-empty outputFile value. The implementation (lines 885-909 in protheus_preset.go) handles file writing correctly, but lacks unit test coverage for this specific feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Connection resolution priority pattern (--connection flag > preset.connection > active profile) is implemented consistently but not documented in AGENTS.md. This pattern may be reused by future features that need connection context.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: protheus_preset.go resolveConnection() function (line 927) and subsequent profile resolution logic (lines 792-834)

sharedStateObservations.observation: The pattern of mapping InterpolationError types to CLI error codes provides a clean separation between interpolation logic and CLI presentation. This could be documented as a reference for similar error handling scenarios.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: protheus_preset.go lines 753-789 - switch on ierr.Type to produce MISSING_PARAMETER, TYPE_MISMATCH, SQL_INJECTION_DETECTED errors

summary: PASS - The preset run command is fully implemented and tested. All 13 expected behaviors are covered. One non-blocking issue found: missing test for --output-file flag. The implementation correctly follows AGENTS.md conventions for output format and error handling. Worker followed the preset-worker skill procedure correctly.
